overall_structure.description: The overall structure is a truss bridge designed to span a 10-unit gap along the y-axis. It consists of a lower deck platform that is 3 units wide in x, 11 units long in y, and 1 unit high in z, paired with an upper chord beam that is 1 unit wide in x, 11 units long in y, and 1 unit high in z. The upper chord is positioned parallel to and above the lower deck, centered in x, with a separation of 2 units in z. The two sub-structures are connected using multiple braces to form a truss, including vertical braces between aligned positions and diagonal braces between offset positions along the y-axis for structural reinforcement.
overall_structure.functionality: The truss bridge rests its ends on the two terrains, spanning the 10-unit gap without fixed connections. The lower deck provides a wide, flat surface to support the 2.5 × 2.5 × 1.5 cargo at the center. The truss design, with the lower deck in tension, upper chord in compression, and braces handling shear forces, distributes the cargo's weight efficiently, allowing the bridge to support increasing loads until structural limits are reached.
overall_structure.assembly: Position the lower deck sub-structure horizontally across the gap along the y-axis. Position the upper chord sub-structure parallel and above it, centered in the x-direction, with a 2-unit separation in the z-direction. Connect them using braces: attach vertical braces between each block in the upper chord and the corresponding middle-row block in the lower deck, and add diagonal braces connecting each upper block to the adjacent lower blocks (one position ahead and behind) in the middle row.
overall_structure.motion_control: N/A - The structure is static and not expected to move.

sub_structure_1.name: Lower Deck Platform
sub_structure_1.description: A flat, rectangular platform composed of small wooden blocks arranged in a 3-wide (x), 11-long (y), 1-high (z) grid to form a continuous surface for spanning the gap and supporting the cargo.
sub_structure_1.components: 1 starting stone block, 32 small wooden blocks.
sub_structure_1.assembly: Start with the starting stone block positioned at the center row, one end of the y-axis (e.g., y = -5, x = 0, z = 5.5). Attach small wooden blocks face-to-face along the y-direction to form the center row of 11 blocks total. Then, attach additional small wooden blocks to the sides (in the x-direction) of the center row to form two adjacent parallel rows, each 11 blocks long, resulting in a 3x11 grid.
sub_structure_1.function: Serves as the primary spanning element and load-bearing platform for the cargo, forming the lower chord of the truss in tension.
sub_structure_1.design_requirements: The platform must be flat, continuous, and without gaps to stably support the cargo. All blocks must be directly attached face-to-face where adjacent. Positions may be micro-adjusted in later stages to resolve conflicts based on actual build execution.

sub_structure_2.name: Upper Chord Beam
sub_structure_2.description: A straight linear beam composed of small wooden blocks chained along the y-axis to span 11 units, providing compressive strength in the truss design.
sub_structure_2.components: 1 starting stone block, 10 small wooden blocks.
sub_structure_2.assembly: Start with the starting stone block at one end (e.g., y = -5, x = 0, z = 7.5). Attach small wooden blocks sequentially face-to-face along the positive y-direction to form a continuous chain of 11 blocks total.
sub_structure_2.function: Acts as the upper chord of the truss, resisting compression forces and enhancing overall structural integrity to support heavier loads.
sub_structure_2.design_requirements: The beam must be straight and linearly aligned along the y-axis. Positions may be micro-adjusted in later stages to resolve conflicts based on actual build execution.
